meta.description: Composes Discussion sections around key findings, mechanisms, clinical relevance, and limitations. Polished: citation placeholder density rule (max 4 per 400 words) added; discussion length calibration by manuscript type added; dead references removed.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated citations, studies, or findings detected. Hard rules mandate citation placeholders when prior literature is not provided.

practice_boundaries.detail: No clinical recommendations beyond what evidence explicitly supports. Skill scope is discussion prose writing only.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies. Limitations rule (constraint → impact → mitigation) enforces honest and proportionate acknowledgment.

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Mode A text-output skill.

functional_suitability.note: 6-part structure comprehensive; citation placeholder rule and limitations constraint→impact→mitigation format are practically valuable; no handling for multi-study manuscripts or meta-analysis discussion structure.

reliability.note: Step 3 revision checklist with 8 verifiable criteria is strong; citation placeholder explicitly prevents fabrication; ask-before-writing rule well-enforced.

performance_context.note: Clean 4-step workflow with 3-part output (draft + placeholder note + assumptions); minor verbosity in optional inputs list.

agent_usability.note: Good sample triggers, 6-part template, and citation placeholder mechanism; feedback design via 3-part output and Step 3 checklist effective.

human_usability.note: Rich trigger phrase list; scope boundary (not Introduction, Methods, or Results) clearly stated.

security.note: Full marks. Hard rules prohibit fabricated citations, invented prior studies, and clinical recommendations beyond evidence.

maintainability.note: Only audit-reference.md present (an audit config, not a modular rule file); dead references to references/guide.md and references/examples/ directory; all rules embedded inline in SKILL.md with no modular structure.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and citation placeholder composability strong; dead reference assets reduce maintainability and confidence in completeness.

inputs.label: Retrospective cohort study: HR 1.43 (95% CI 1.12-1.82), one prior study provided for comparison
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Complete 6-part discussion with correct citation, hedged language, and limitations in required format.

assertions.text: Output follows 6-part discussion structure (opening, interpretation, literature comparison, implications, limitations, conclusion)
assertions.note: All 6 parts present and in correct order.

assertions.text: Provided prior study is cited and compared, not ignored
assertions.note: Literature comparison paragraph directly engages the user-provided prior study.

assertions.text: Limitations are formatted as constraint → impact on interpretation → mitigation/future direction
assertions.note: Limitations paragraph follows the required 3-part format.

assertions.text: Hedged language ('suggests', 'may reflect', 'is consistent with') used throughout interpretation
assertions.note: No inappropriate causal language in interpretation section.

assertions.text: No new data or results introduced in the Discussion that were not in the user's provided results
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced; discussion built only on user-provided findings.

inputs.label: Key findings provided but no prior literature — skill must use citation placeholders throughout
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Citation placeholders used correctly; placeholder density slightly high for the discussion length.

assertions.text: Output uses [CITE: ...] placeholders rather than invented citations
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced; no fabricated author names, journals, or findings.

assertions.text: Citation placeholders are specific enough to guide the user's literature search
assertions.note: Placeholders describe the type of study needed (e.g., '[CITE: cohort study reporting similar association in [population]]').

assertions.text: Step 4 output explicitly notes all placeholders inserted and how many need to be filled
assertions.note: Placeholder summary note provided as required.

assertions.text: Placeholder density is proportionate to discussion length (not excessive relative to prose)
assertions.note: 7 citation placeholders in a 380-word discussion creates a placeholder-heavy output that may read as incomplete rather than a starting draft. Guideline needed for max placeholder density.

assertions.text: Discussion remains readable and interpretable despite placeholders
assertions.note: Prose structure and argument logic are intact despite placeholder presence.

inputs.label: Minimal input: 'Write my discussion' — no findings, no research question
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Input collection triggered correctly; no discussion drafted.

assertions.text: Skill does not draft discussion without key results and research question
Output asks specifically for primary finding (with quantitative detail) and research question
assertions.note: Questions target the mandatory inputs named in Step 1.

assertions.text: Output also asks about tone/depth preference (brief vs full discussion)
Output does not invent findings or fabricate a placeholder discussion
assertions.note: Hard rules enforced; no invented content.

assertions.text: Output communicates the 6-part structure that will be applied once inputs are provided
assertions.note: User informed of expected output structure.

key_strengths: Citation placeholder rule ([CITE: study showing...]) prevents fabrication while keeping the discussion structurally complete — a practical and safe design choice for a writing skill
Limitations format (constraint → impact → mitigation) provides concrete structural guidance that prevents the common failure of listing limitations without explanation
Step 3 revision checklist with 8 verifiable criteria enables systematic self-audit before delivery
Clear distinction between Discussion scope and other section types (Introduction, Methods, Results) prevents scope creep

recommendations.title: Dead references to guide.md, examples/ directory, and scripts/main.py in SKILL.md
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md references references/guide.md, references/examples/, and (via audit-reference.md) scripts/main.py — none of these exist. The missing guide.md was presumably intended to hold detailed examples, style guidance, and templates. Without it, the skill lacks supplementary reference material.
recommendations.root_cause: Files referenced during authoring but never created; no file existence validation at deployment.
recommendations.fix: Create references/guide.md with: (1) 2 annotated example discussion paragraphs for different study types, (2) a hedging language vocabulary list, (3) a citation placeholder format standard. Remove the reference to scripts/main.py or create the script.

recommendations.title: No guidance on maximum citation placeholder density
recommendations.problem: When users provide no prior literature, the skill generates many citation placeholders, which can make a 400-word draft feel incomplete and unreadable. No density limit is defined.
recommendations.root_cause: Literature comparison rules require placeholders for all literature comparison points but set no upper bound on placeholder count.
recommendations.fix: Add a placeholder density rule: 'Limit citation placeholders to a maximum of 4 per 400 words of discussion. For additional comparison points, note at the end of the section: [Additional literature comparison points identified — user to supply 2-3 relevant studies for the following claims: ...] rather than inserting inline placeholders for every comparison.'

recommendations.title: No discussion length calibration by manuscript type or journal tier
recommendations.problem: The skill asks for brief (3-4 paragraphs) vs full (6+ paragraphs) but does not map these to specific manuscript types or journal expectations. A Nature letter discussion is 1-2 paragraphs; an original research article in a specialty journal may be 600-900 words.
recommendations.root_cause: Tone/depth input in Step 1 is open-ended without examples.
recommendations.fix: Add length calibration examples to Step 1: 'Brief (3–4 paragraphs, ~300–400 words): short communications, case reports, letters. Standard (5–6 paragraphs, ~500–700 words): original research articles in specialty journals. Extended (7+ paragraphs, ~800–1000 words): high-impact journals, multi-finding studies, studies with substantial prior literature to engage.'
